summary: A symmetrical ceremonial or administrative building featuring a central tower with a wide base structure. The design includes a prominent central vertical element (tower) with a flat top platform, flanked by two rectangular wings. The structure is elevated with multiple material layers creating a tiered appearance. White fence posts form a perimeter boundary around the entire complex.

shape.overall_form: Cross or T-shaped with central tower

rebuild_hints: Build central tower first as vertical spine, approximately 2-3 blocks wide
Create tiered base with alternating material layers (brick, stone, wood, hay)
Construct two symmetrical rectangular wings on either side of tower
Add horizontal wood beam details across all walls at regular intervals
Place white fence posts in a rectangular perimeter around entire structure
Top tower with flat platform and decorative gold block accents
Ensure strict bilateral symmetry along the north-south axis
Layer materials horizontally: brick primary, stone bands, wood trim, hay fill
uncertainties: Exact block count per layer due to perspective distortion
Interior layout and room divisions not visible from exterior views
Precise height of each tier - estimated from visual proportions
Whether hay_block or similar is used for tan/yellow layers
Exact positioning of gold block decorations on tower
Whether structure has a basement or foundation below visible level
